title: Fixing Middleware Order for an Express API

statement.markdown: Implement an Express API that handles GET requests to the root URL ('/'). The API should return a JSON response with the status code and a message. The middleware should be correctly ordered to handle the request correctly.
statement.inputFormat: The input is not used in this problem.
statement.outputFormat: The output should be a JSON response with the status code and a message.
statement.notes: The middleware should be ordered correctly to handle the request.

constraints.description: The number of requests to handle.

examples.explanation: The API should return a JSON response with the status code and a message.

hints.text: Check the order of the middleware.

editorial.title: Editorial Title
editorial.intuition: The middleware should be ordered such that it handles the request correctly.
editorial.bruteForce: The brute force approach would be to try different orders of the middleware.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution is to use the correct order of the middleware.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The proof of correctness is that the API returns the correct response.
editorial.dryRun: The dry run would be to try different orders of the middleware and see which one works.
